truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 findings against 20 true vulnerabilities. Run 2 identified 14 findings. Of the 20 true vulnerabilities, 10 were fully or partially found, and 10 were missed. Several findings had severity or CWE misclassifications. No findings were false positives — all were supported by the source, though some captured related but distinct issues from the truth list.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Format String Vulnerability in Log Function" — evidence: "printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format-string injection via runtime_log_format() with printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input). Severity was listed as High; truth is Critical.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A — no finding in Run 2 corresponds to integer overflow in factorial computation
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the integer overflow in the fact() lambda (result *= i on long long without bounds check). Completely absent from findings.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Use-After-Free in Deferred History Flush" — evidence: "char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1)); ... thread_pool_->enqueue([scratch, copy_len] { ... });"
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the UAF between stage_deferred_history_flush and g_memory_pool.cleanup with scratch memory passed to thread pool. Severity was High; truth is Critical.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Command Injection via Config Validation" — evidence: "std::string command = validator_cmd + \" \" + config_file;\nint result = system(command.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified command injection via validate_config. Severity Critical matches truth.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression" — evidence: "if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) {\n    strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow via strcpy when length >= buffer size. Severity High matches truth.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Race Condition in ThreadSafeCounter" — evidence: "long long old_value = value_;\nstd::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);\nvalue_ = old_value + 1;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the race condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment. Severity Medium matches truth.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Backdoor Authentication Mechanism" — evidence: "if (login_attempts_ > 5) {\n    if (input.find(\"EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE\") != std::string::npos) {\n        authenticated_ = true;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the logic bomb/backdoor code but classified it as CWE-489 (Backdoor) instead of truth's CWE-511 (Misused Controls / Logic Bomb). The code match is exact.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Path Traversal in Config Loading" — evidence: "std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;\nif (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified path traversal in load_config. Severity Medium matches truth.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A — no finding in Run 2 corresponds to heap overflow in ExpressionParser with strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str())
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the heap buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse() where strcpy is used into a 256-byte pool-allocated buffer without length check. Completely absent.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Hardcoded Admin Credentials" — evidence: "constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = \"SC_ENT_2025_AUTH\";"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified hardcoded credentials. Severity High matches truth.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Weak Session Token Generation" — evidence: "std::mt19937 gen(rd());\nstd::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified weak PRNG for session tokens but used CWE-330 (Weak Cryptography) instead of truth's CWE-338 (Weak PRNG). The vulnerability is correctly identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Unsafe localtime Usage" — evidence: "static std::tm cached_time;\nif (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;"
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the thread-unsafe localtime caching but classified it as CWE-479 (Use of Obsolete Function) instead of truth's CWE-362 (Data Race).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A — Run 2's 'Command Injection in Override Audit' finding addresses command injection, not specifically insecure temporary file handling (CWE-377)
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the insecure temporary file creation via TEMP/TMPDIR env vars and system() for supercalc_emergency.log as a distinct CWE-377 issue.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A — no finding in Run 2 corresponds to persistent authentication state (CWE-613)
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify that authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ persist across sessions without proper invalidation.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A — no finding in Run 2 corresponds to unhandled exception in std::stod(tokens[3])
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the unhandled exception risk when std::stod is called on tokens[3] in handle_variable_command without try/catch at that call site.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A — no finding in Run 2 corresponds to integer underflow in memory-pool block split
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the integer underflow when previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow if previous_size is small.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A — no finding in Run 2 corresponds to unbounded recursion in parse_expression/parse_factor with PAREN_OPEN
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the unbounded recursion risk when nested parentheses trigger recursive parse_expression calls without depth limiting.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A — no finding in Run 2 corresponds to data race in result_cache_ accessed without cache_mutex_ in evaluate_cached
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify that result_cache_ is accessed in evaluate_cached without holding cache_mutex_, creating a data race when thread_pool_->enqueue calls evaluate_cached concurrently.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: N/A — Run 2's path traversal finding does not address the TOCTOU between std::filesystem::exists and std::ifstream open with sleep_for in between
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the TOCTOU race in load_config where sleep_for(25ms) occurs between existence check and file open.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "Division by Zero in calculate_route_score" — evidence: "return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified division by zero in calculate_route_score. Severity was listed as Medium; truth is Low.

buffer overflow in result formatting|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|0.85|enhanced_calc.cpp|691|694|calculator::supercalc::run|char formatted_result[64]; snprintf(formatted_result, sizeof(formatted_result), "%.10g", result); sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|sprintf without bounds checking can overflow result_buffer_ if formatted_result is large|calculation results that produce long formatted strings|use snprintf with sizeof(result_buffer_) for the sprintf call->buffer overflow in result formatting|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|0.85|enhanced_calc.cpp|691|694|calculator::supercalc::run|char formatted_result[64]; snprintf(formatted_result, sizeof(formatted_result), "%.10g", result); sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|sprintf without bounds checking can overflow result_buffer_ if formatted_result is large|calculation results that produce long formatted strings|use snprintf with sizeof(result_buffer_) for the sprintf call
